info.name: iD+ Platform API — Complete
info.description:
Complete Postman collection for the iD+ platform Laravel API (https://idplus.cfd/ID-platform). Covers every route registered under `api/v1` as of 2026-07-10 (verified against `php artisan route:list`).

**Getting started**: run 'Auth → Login — Superadmin' (or 'Login — Employee (mobile)' for the Mobile folder). The login test script stores `data.token` into the `{{token}}` collection variable; the collection-level Bearer auth applies it everywhere except the Public folder.

**Variables**: `base_url` (https://idplus.cfd/ID-platform/api/v1), `token` (auto-filled by login), `device_token` (any string; default `flutter_dev_token`).

**Response envelope**: every JSON endpoint wraps its payload as `{"success": bool, "message": string, "data": mixed}`.

**QUIRKS**:
- Most validation failures return **HTTP 422 with `success: true` and `data: null`** — read `message` for the error text; do NOT trust `success` alone.
- Exceptions: `CompanyRequest` (company store/update) and `auth/forgot-password` return `success: false` plus an `errors` object on validation failure.
- `privacy-policy` / `terms-conditions` return **HTTP 400** on a bad `lang` value.
- The WAF in front of the server rejects empty POST bodies — every otherwise-empty POST in this collection sends a raw `{}` JSON body on purpose.
- `dashboard/business-cards/{id}/analytics` is a **POST**, not a GET.
- `PUT dashboard/employee-project/{id}` is registered in the router but the controller has **no `update` method** — calling it 500s. Marked DEPRECATED/BROKEN below.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Authentication endpoints (Laravel Sanctum bearer tokens).

Run one of the two Login requests first — a test script captures `data.token` into the collection variable `{{token}}`, which the collection-level Bearer auth then applies to every protected request.

**Response envelope**: every JSON endpoint wraps its payload as `{"success": bool, "message": string, "data": mixed}`.

**QUIRKS**:
- Most validation failures return **HTTP 422 with `success: true` and `data: null`** — read `message` for the error text; do NOT trust `success` alone.
- Exceptions: `CompanyRequest` (company store/update) and `auth/forgot-password` return `success: false` plus an `errors` object on validation failure.
- `privacy-policy` / `terms-conditions` return **HTTP 400** on a bad `lang` value.
- The WAF in front of the server rejects empty POST bodies — every otherwise-empty POST in this collection sends a raw `{}` JSON body on purpose.
- `dashboard/business-cards/{id}/analytics` is a **POST**, not a GET.
- `PUT dashboard/employee-project/{id}` is registered in the router but the controller has **no `update` method** — calling it 500s. Marked DEPRECATED/BROKEN below.

request.description: Login as the seeded superadmin. `device_token` accepts any string (FCM token in production). On success the test script stores `data.token` into the collection variable `{{token}}` so every bearer request works immediately.
Login as the demo employee account used by the mobile app. The test script stores `data.token` into `{{token}}`. Use this before exercising the Mobile folder.
Sends a password-reset OTP to the email. QUIRK: unlike most endpoints, validation failures here return `success: false` with an `errors` object.
Completes the reset started by Forgot Password using the emailed OTP.
Sends a phone OTP to the given number.
Verifies the 4-digit phone OTP sent by Send OTP.
Returns the authenticated user's profile (user, roles/permissions, employee + company/branch/department relations where applicable). Bearer token required.
Revokes the current bearer token. Sends `{}` because the WAF rejects empty POST bodies.

item.name: Dashboard
item.description:
Dashboard (admin) API — bearer token required. Role gate: **superadmin|owner** on almost everything (Companies are superadmin-only; `owner/company` is owner-only) plus per-action Spatie permissions noted on each request.

**Response envelope**: every JSON endpoint wraps its payload as `{"success": bool, "message": string, "data": mixed}`.

**QUIRKS**:
- Most validation failures return **HTTP 422 with `success: true` and `data: null`** — read `message` for the error text; do NOT trust `success` alone.
- Exceptions: `CompanyRequest` (company store/update) and `auth/forgot-password` return `success: false` plus an `errors` object on validation failure.
- `privacy-policy` / `terms-conditions` return **HTTP 400** on a bad `lang` value.
- The WAF in front of the server rejects empty POST bodies — every otherwise-empty POST in this collection sends a raw `{}` JSON body on purpose.
- `dashboard/business-cards/{id}/analytics` is a **POST**, not a GET.
- `PUT dashboard/employee-project/{id}` is registered in the router but the controller has **no `update` method** — calling it 500s. Marked DEPRECATED/BROKEN below.

item.name: Companies
item.description: Company CRUD — **superadmin role only** (plus per-action `company.*` Spatie permissions). Store/update are multipart because of the required `logo` image. QUIRK: `CompanyRequest` validation failures return `success: false` with an `errors` object (unlike the 422-with-success:true pattern elsewhere).

request.description: List all companies. Superadmin only (`company.view`).
Create a company (multipart/form-data — `logo` image file is REQUIRED). `user_id` must reference an owner-type user. Superadmin only (`company.create`).

formdata.description: ID of an existing owner-type user who owns this company
REQUIRED — company logo image file (jpg/png). Attach a file before sending.

request.description: Show one company. Superadmin only (`company.view`).

Update a company (multipart, `logo` image required). NOTE: PHP cannot parse multipart bodies on PUT, so this request uses POST with a `_method=PUT` spoof field (Laravel method spoofing) — it hits the registered PUT|PATCH route. Superadmin only (`company.update`).

formdata.description: Laravel method spoof — targets the PUT|PATCH route

request.description: Delete a company. Superadmin only (`company.delete`).
Returns the company owned by the authenticated **owner** user (owner role required — superadmins should use Get Company instead).

item.name: Company Branches
item.description: Branch CRUD — roles superadmin|owner + `company_branch.*` permissions. JSON bodies.

request.description: List branches (`company_branch.view`).
Create a branch. `address` is optional (`company_branch.create`).
Show one branch (`company_branch.view`).
Update a branch (`company_branch.update`).
Delete a branch (`company_branch.delete`).

item.name: Departments
item.description: Department CRUD — roles superadmin|owner + `department.*` permissions. JSON bodies.

request.description: List departments (`department.view`).
Create a department. `code` is optional (`department.create`).
Show one department (`department.view`).
Update a department (`department.update`).
Delete a department (`department.delete`).

item.name: Employees
item.description: Employee CRUD — roles superadmin|owner + `employee.*` permissions. Store/update are multipart because of the optional `logo` photo field. `user_id` and `employee_number` are now OPTIONAL on create (server auto-creates the user / auto-numbers `EMP-{company}-NNNN`).

request.description: List employees (`employee.view`).

Create an employee (multipart — optional `logo` photo). NEW BEHAVIOUR: `user_id` is now OPTIONAL (the server creates the linked user when absent) and `employee_number` is now OPTIONAL (auto-generated as `EMP-{company}-NNNN`). `status` must be `active` or `inactive`. Requires `employee.create`.

formdata.description: Optional — Spatie role ID to assign
OPTIONAL — omit and the server creates the user
Optional — only used when the server creates the linked user

request.description: Show one employee (`employee.view`).

Update an employee (multipart, `logo` optional). NOTE: PHP cannot parse multipart bodies on PUT, so this request uses POST with a `_method=PUT` spoof field (Laravel method spoofing) — it hits the registered PUT|PATCH route. Requires `employee.update`.

formdata.description: Laravel method spoof — targets the PUT|PATCH route

request.description: Delete an employee (`employee.delete`).

item.name: Projects
item.description: Project CRUD — roles superadmin|owner + `project.*` permissions. JSON bodies. `employee_ids[]` on store/update syncs assignments.

request.description: List projects (`project.view`).
Create a project. `start_date`, `end_date`, `employee_ids[]` optional (`project.create`).
Show one project (`project.view`).
Update a project (`project.update`).
Delete a project (`project.delete`).

item.name: Employee-Project Assignments
item.description: Pivot CRUD linking employees to projects — roles superadmin|owner + `employee_project.*` permissions. QUIRK: the PUT route is registered but the controller has NO `update` method — it is DEPRECATED/BROKEN (500s). Delete + re-create instead.

request.description: List employee-project assignments. Supports `employee_id` / `project_id` query filters (`employee_project.view`).
Assign an employee to a project (`employee_project.create`).
Show one assignment (`employee_project.view`).

DEPRECATED / BROKEN — the route is registered in the router but `EmployeeProjectController` has NO `update` method, so this call fails server-side. Kept only for route-coverage completeness. Use Delete + Create Assignment instead. Flagged for removal from the backend.
Remove an employee from a project (`employee_project.delete`).

item.name: Business Card Templates
item.description: Card template CRUD — roles superadmin|owner + `business_card_template.*` permissions. JSON bodies.

request.description: List templates (`business_card_template.view`).
Create a template. `design_json` and `is_default` optional (`business_card_template.create`).
Show one template (`business_card_template.view`).
Update a template (`business_card_template.update`).
Delete a template (`business_card_template.delete`).

item.name: Business Cards
item.description:
Business card CRUD + lifecycle — roles superadmin|owner + `business_card.*` permissions.

Lifecycle: Create (bulk via `employee_ids[]`) → Submit (to employee for approval) → employee Approves/Rejects via Mobile folder → Publish → Deactivate.

QUIRKS: `/{id}/analytics` is a **POST**, not a GET. Empty-body lifecycle POSTs send `{}` because the WAF rejects empty POST bodies. `expiry_days` on create is undocumented and defaults to 2.

request.description: List business cards (`business_card.view`).
Create cards for one or more employees. `nfc_code`, `is_active` optional; `expiry_days` is undocumented and defaults to 2 (`business_card.create`).
Show one card (`business_card.view`).
Update a card (`business_card.update`).
Delete a card (superadmin|owner role; no per-action permission on this route).
Moves the card to the employee-approval stage (`business_card.submit`). Sends `{}` — WAF rejects empty POST bodies.
Publishes an approved card (`business_card.publish`). Sends `{}` — WAF rejects empty POST bodies.
Deactivates a published card (`business_card.deactivate`). Sends `{}` — WAF rejects empty POST bodies.
Records an interaction against the card (superadmin|owner). `interaction_type` required (e.g. view, save_contact, share); `source` optional but recommended (e.g. qr, nfc, link).
Returns interaction analytics for the card. QUIRK: this is a **POST**, not a GET. Sends `{}` — WAF rejects empty POST bodies (superadmin|owner).

item.name: Roles & Permissions
item.description: Spatie role CRUD — roles superadmin|owner + `role.*` permissions. `permissions[]` uses permission names like `employee.view`.

request.description: List roles with their permissions (`role.view`).
Create a role. `permissions[]` optional — permission name strings (`role.create`).
Show one role (`role.view`).
Update a role and sync its permissions (`role.update`).
Delete a role (`role.delete`).
Bulk-assigns the role to the given users (superadmin|owner).

item.name: Users
item.description: User registration from the dashboard — roles superadmin|owner.

request.description: Creates a user account. `user_type` must be one of `owner | employee | superadmin`. Requires superadmin|owner role.

item.name: Mobile (Employee)
item.description:
Mobile app endpoints — bearer token required, roles **superadmin|employee** + `business_card.*` permissions. Log in with 'Login — Employee (mobile)' first. The employee sees cards authored for them by the admin and approves/rejects them.

**Response envelope**: every JSON endpoint wraps its payload as `{"success": bool, "message": string, "data": mixed}`.

**QUIRKS**:
- Most validation failures return **HTTP 422 with `success: true` and `data: null`** — read `message` for the error text; do NOT trust `success` alone.
- Exceptions: `CompanyRequest` (company store/update) and `auth/forgot-password` return `success: false` plus an `errors` object on validation failure.
- `privacy-policy` / `terms-conditions` return **HTTP 400** on a bad `lang` value.
- The WAF in front of the server rejects empty POST bodies — every otherwise-empty POST in this collection sends a raw `{}` JSON body on purpose.
- `dashboard/business-cards/{id}/analytics` is a **POST**, not a GET.
- `PUT dashboard/employee-project/{id}` is registered in the router but the controller has **no `update` method** — calling it 500s. Marked DEPRECATED/BROKEN below.

request.description: Lists the authenticated employee's business cards (`business_card.view`).
Shows one of the employee's cards (`business_card.view`).
Employee approves a submitted card (`business_card.approve`). Sends `{}` — WAF rejects empty POST bodies.
Employee rejects a submitted card with a reason (`business_card.reject`).

item.name: Notifications
item.description:
In-app notifications — bearer token required, any authenticated role.

**Response envelope**: every JSON endpoint wraps its payload as `{"success": bool, "message": string, "data": mixed}`.

**QUIRKS**:
- Most validation failures return **HTTP 422 with `success: true` and `data: null`** — read `message` for the error text; do NOT trust `success` alone.
- Exceptions: `CompanyRequest` (company store/update) and `auth/forgot-password` return `success: false` plus an `errors` object on validation failure.
- `privacy-policy` / `terms-conditions` return **HTTP 400** on a bad `lang` value.
- The WAF in front of the server rejects empty POST bodies — every otherwise-empty POST in this collection sends a raw `{}` JSON body on purpose.
- `dashboard/business-cards/{id}/analytics` is a **POST**, not a GET.
- `PUT dashboard/employee-project/{id}` is registered in the router but the controller has **no `update` method** — calling it 500s. Marked DEPRECATED/BROKEN below.

request.description: Lists the authenticated user's notifications.
Marks one notification as read. Sends `{}` — WAF rejects empty POST bodies.

item.name: Public (No Auth)
item.description:
Public endpoints — **no authentication** (this folder overrides the collection bearer auth with No Auth).

QUIRKS: `card/{slug}` returns an HTML page and `vcard` returns a .vcf file (not the JSON envelope). `privacy-policy`/`terms-conditions` return HTTP 400 on a bad `lang`. Always send `source` on the public track call.

**Response envelope**: every JSON endpoint wraps its payload as `{"success": bool, "message": string, "data": mixed}`.

**QUIRKS**:
- Most validation failures return **HTTP 422 with `success: true` and `data: null`** — read `message` for the error text; do NOT trust `success` alone.
- Exceptions: `CompanyRequest` (company store/update) and `auth/forgot-password` return `success: false` plus an `errors` object on validation failure.
- `privacy-policy` / `terms-conditions` return **HTTP 400** on a bad `lang` value.
- The WAF in front of the server rejects empty POST bodies — every otherwise-empty POST in this collection sends a raw `{}` JSON body on purpose.
- `dashboard/business-cards/{id}/analytics` is a **POST**, not a GET.
- `PUT dashboard/employee-project/{id}` is registered in the router but the controller has **no `update` method** — calling it 500s. Marked DEPRECATED/BROKEN below.

variable.description: Card slug from the card's public URL / NFC tag

request.description: Public business-card landing page. Returns **HTML**, not the JSON envelope.
Downloads the card as a .vcf vCard file (save-to-contacts). Returns a file, not the JSON envelope.
Public card data as JSON (used by the web card viewer).
Records a public interaction. `interaction_type` required (view, save_contact, share, ...). **Always send `source`** (qr | nfc | link) — omitting it has caused server errors.

query.description: ar | en — QUIRK: bad value returns HTTP 400

request.description: Localized privacy policy. QUIRK: invalid `lang` returns HTTP 400 (not 422).

query.description: ar | en — QUIRK: bad value returns HTTP 400

request.description: Localized terms & conditions. QUIRK: invalid `lang` returns HTTP 400 (not 422).
App-wide constants/enums used by the mobile and web clients.
Public contact information.
DEV ONLY — sends a test FCM push from a route closure. Not for production use; **flagged for removal** from the backend.
